steps.title: Recognize what the constant term represents
steps.content: In any function h(t), when we set t = 0, all terms containing t vanish and only the constant term remains. Since t = 0 represents the launch moment, the constant term gives the initial height. In $h(t) = -16t^2 + 96t + 84$, the constant term is 84.

steps.title: Evaluate h(0) to find the initial height
steps.content:
$$h(0) = -16(0)^2 + 96(0) + 84 = 84 \text{ feet}$$
At the moment of launch (t = 0), the object is at a height of 84 feet above the ground. This is the initial height of the object. That confirms A is correct.
